text: MEXT Scholarship (Japan). The Japanese Government MEXT Scholarship supports international students who wish to study in Japan at Japanese universities and other institutions. MEXT has several categories, including undergraduate students, research students, teacher training students, Japanese studies students, college of technology students, specialized training college students, and Young Leaders Program students. Research student applicants generally need to be eligible for admission to a Japanese graduate school and meet age and academic requirements. Applicants usually apply through an embassy recommendation or university recommendation route.
language_details: Japanese or English depending on program and university
funding_details: Typically covers tuition, monthly stipend, and travel support.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Generally open to Mongolian applicants or international applicants, but applicants should verify current official eligibility rules.

text: Australia Awards (Australia). Australia Awards Scholarships provide citizens of Mongolia and other participating countries with opportunities to obtain postgraduate qualifications at Australian tertiary institutions. The program is a bilateral development scholarship and is designed to build skills and knowledge that recipients can use to contribute to development in their home country. Applicants should check the Mongolia-specific Australia Awards website for eligibility, priority fields, English requirements, work experience requirements, and return-home obligations.
language_details: English proficiency required (IELTS/TOEFL typically required)
funding_details: Typically covers tuition, return travel, establishment allowance, living allowance, health cover, and academic support.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Generally open to Mongolian applicants or international applicants, but applicants should verify current official eligibility rules.

text: Chevening Scholarship (United Kingdom). Chevening Scholarships enable outstanding emerging leaders from around the world to pursue one-year master's degrees in the United Kingdom. Applicants usually need strong academic preparation, leadership potential, and a clear plan for using the UK education to address local, national, or global challenges. Chevening is relevant for students seeking fully funded master's study in the UK, especially those with leadership experience and career goals connected to public service, development, policy, business, or civil society.
language_details: English proficiency required (IELTS/TOEFL typically required)
funding_details: Typically covers tuition, travel costs, visa costs, and monthly living allowance.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Generally open to Mongolian applicants or international applicants, but applicants should verify current official eligibility rules.

text: DAAD Scholarships (Germany). DAAD provides scholarship programmes for international students, graduates, doctoral students, postdocs, and researchers who want to study or conduct research in Germany. DAAD scholarships vary by programme, field, degree level, and applicant country. Some programmes support master's study, some support doctoral research, and some focus on development-related postgraduate courses. Applicants should use the DAAD scholarship database to check eligible programmes, academic requirements, language requirements, funding duration, and application documents.
language_details: Depends on program (German or English requirements vary)
funding_details: Funding depends on the specific DAAD programme; many include monthly stipend, insurance, travel allowance, and sometimes tuition-related support.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Generally open to Mongolian applicants or international applicants, but applicants should verify current official eligibility rules.

text: Erasmus Mundus Joint Master Degrees (European Union). Erasmus Mundus Joint Masters are prestigious international master's programmes jointly designed and delivered by groups of higher education institutions. They usually involve study in at least two countries and may include study, research, traineeship, thesis preparation, and defense. Scholarships may be available for selected students.
language_details: Depends on specific program and universities
funding_details: Scholarships for selected students typically cover participation costs, travel contribution, and monthly living allowance.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Generally open to Mongolian applicants or international applicants, but applicants should verify current official eligibility rules.

text: Swiss Government Excellence Scholarships (Switzerland). The Swiss Government Excellence Scholarships are mainly intended for talented early-career researchers from abroad who have already completed a master's degree or PhD. They support postgraduate research at Swiss universities, universities of applied sciences, federal institutes of technology, or research institutes. Research applicants generally need support from an academic supervisor in Switzerland.
language_details: Depends on host institution and research supervisor requirements
funding_details: Typically provides monthly stipend, health insurance, and support for research stay; exact benefits vary by scholarship type.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Generally open to Mongolian applicants or international applicants, but applicants should verify current official eligibility rules.

text: Türkiye Scholarships (Turkey). Türkiye Scholarships is a government-funded scholarship programme for international students. It offers opportunities for associate, bachelor's, master's, PhD, research, and other scholarship categories. The programme is designed as a comprehensive scholarship that may include university placement, tuition support, accommodation, health insurance, and academic, social, and cultural activities. Applicants must meet academic and age criteria for their intended degree level and apply through the official Türkiye Scholarships portal.
language_details: Turkish or English depending on program; language training may be provided
funding_details: Typically covers tuition, monthly stipend, accommodation, health insurance, Turkish language course, and travel support.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Generally open to Mongolian applicants or international applicants, but applicants should verify current official eligibility rules.

text: Chinese Government Scholarship (China). The Chinese Government Scholarship supports international students studying in China. For Mongolia-specific embassy announcements, eligibility may include Mongolian citizenship, health requirements, degree-level age limits, and academic background requirements such as high school graduation for undergraduate study, bachelor's degree for master's study, and master's degree for doctoral study. Applicants should check the Chinese Embassy in Mongolia and the official CSC study-in-China application system for current eligibility and procedures.
language_details: Chinese or English depending on program and university
funding_details: Usually covers tuition, accommodation, stipend, and medical insurance; exact coverage depends on scholarship category.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Generally open to Mongolian applicants or international applicants, but applicants should verify current official eligibility rules.

text: Korean Government Scholarship Program / Global Korea Scholarship (South Korea). The Global Korea Scholarship is a Korean government scholarship programme designed to invite outstanding international students to study at higher education institutions in Korea. Selected students may receive support such as airfare, Korean language training, tuition, and a monthly allowance. Applicants may apply through an embassy track or university track depending on the programme and application cycle.
language_details: Korean or English depending on program; language training often provided
funding_details: Typically covers airfare, tuition, settlement allowance, monthly allowance, Korean language training, and insurance.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Generally open to Mongolian applicants or international applicants, but applicants should verify current official eligibility rules.

text: Joint Japan/World Bank Graduate Scholarship Program (JJ/WBGSP). The Joint Japan/World Bank Graduate Scholarship Program supports citizens of eligible developing countries with relevant professional experience who are applying to approved master's programmes in development-related fields. The programme focuses on areas such as economic policy, tax policy, infrastructure management, and other development topics. Applicants must usually be admitted to a participating master's programme to be eligible.
scholarship: Joint Japan/World Bank Graduate Scholarship Program
language_details: English proficiency required for participating master's programs
funding_details: Typically covers tuition, monthly living stipend, travel, and health insurance for approved master's programmes.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Generally open to Mongolian applicants or international applicants, but applicants should verify current official eligibility rules.

text: JISPA / Japan-IMF Scholarship Program for Asia. The Japan-IMF Scholarship Program for Asia is designed for junior government officials from key economic agencies in Asia and the Pacific. It provides opportunities to pursue graduate-level degrees in economics at leading Japanese universities and is connected to future leadership in economic policy.
language_details: English proficiency required
funding_details: Typically covers tuition, monthly stipend, travel, and related study expenses.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Generally open to Mongolian applicants or international applicants, but applicants should verify current official eligibility rules.

text: Asian Development Bank–Japan Scholarship Program (ADB-JSP). The ADB-Japan Scholarship Program is open to nationals of ADB borrowing member countries who have gained admission to an approved master's course at a designated institution. It supports postgraduate study in fields related to development, economics, management, science and technology, and other areas relevant to development in Asia and the Pacific.
language_details: English proficiency required
funding_details: Typically covers full tuition, monthly subsistence and housing allowance, books, medical insurance, and travel.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Generally open to Mongolian applicants or international applicants, but applicants should verify current official eligibility rules.

text: United Nations Fellowships. United Nations fellowships are training or fellowship activities designed to build capacity for qualified individuals, often from developing countries or member states. Examples include UN/DESA training and fellowship programmes and the United Nations International Law Fellowship Programme, which provides training for qualified professionals such as government officials and teachers of international law from developing countries and emerging economies.
language_details: Typically requires professional working proficiency in English or other UN languages
funding_details: Funding varies by fellowship; some provide travel, training costs, stipend, or subsistence support.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Generally open to Mongolian applicants or international applicants, but applicants should verify current official eligibility rules.

text: Commonwealth Scholarship and Fellowship Plan. Commonwealth Scholarships support students from eligible Commonwealth countries to pursue postgraduate study, professional development, or research opportunities. Eligibility, participating countries, and degree levels depend on the specific Commonwealth scholarship route. Applicants should check whether their citizenship country is eligible before applying. Mongolia is not a Commonwealth country, so this is usually not a primary option for Mongolian citizens unless a specific route says otherwise.
language_details: English proficiency required
funding_details: Many Commonwealth awards cover tuition, airfare, living allowance, and other approved costs; exact benefits depend on route.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Mongolia is not a Commonwealth country; generally not a primary option unless a specific route says otherwise.

text: Stipendium Hungaricum (Hungary). Stipendium Hungaricum is Hungary's government scholarship programme for international students. It supports full-degree and partial study opportunities in Hungarian higher education institutions, usually through agreements with sending partners in eligible countries. Applicants normally apply through the official application system and the responsible sending partner in their home country.
language_details: Hungarian or English depending on program
funding_details: Typically covers tuition exemption, monthly stipend, accommodation contribution, and medical insurance.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Generally open to Mongolian applicants or international applicants, but applicants should verify current official eligibility rules.

text: Eiffel Excellence Scholarship Program (France). The Eiffel Excellence Scholarship Program is a French government scholarship programme for top international students at master's and doctoral levels. It is designed to help French higher education institutions attract excellent foreign students in priority fields. Applications are usually submitted by French institutions rather than directly by students.
language_details: French or English depending on program
funding_details: Usually provides a monthly allowance and some services; tuition is generally not covered by the Eiffel scholarship itself.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Generally open to Mongolian applicants or international applicants, but applicants should verify current official eligibility rules.

text: Orange Knowledge Programme (Netherlands). The Orange Knowledge Programme was a Dutch scholarship programme focused on strengthening knowledge and capacity through education and training for professionals from selected countries. It supported short courses and master's programmes in the Netherlands for eligible professionals. Applicants should verify the current status of the programme and eligible country list because Dutch scholarship programmes and names can change over time.
language_details: English proficiency typically required for courses
funding_details: Usually provides contribution toward tuition, visa, travel, insurance, and living costs; exact coverage depends on course and institution.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Eligibility depends on current eligible country list and program status.

text: Swedish Institute Scholarships (Sweden). Swedish Institute scholarships support international students applying for eligible master's programmes in Sweden. The Swedish Institute Scholarships for Global Professionals are intended for applicants from selected eligible countries and usually emphasize leadership, professional experience, and contribution to sustainable development. Applicants normally need to apply separately for master's admission and for the scholarship.
language_details: English proficiency required
funding_details: Typically covers tuition, monthly living allowance, insurance, and travel grant for eligible students.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Generally open to Mongolian applicants or international applicants, but applicants should verify current official eligibility rules.

text: New Zealand Scholarships (New Zealand). New Zealand government scholarships have supported students from eligible countries to study in New Zealand or the Pacific, often with a development focus. Eligibility depends heavily on country, priority sector, and scholarship type. Applicants should check the official New Zealand scholarships website for current eligible countries, study levels, and application cycles.
language_details: English proficiency required
funding_details: Typically covers tuition, living allowance, establishment allowance, travel, medical insurance, and other study costs.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Eligibility depends on current country and sector rules.

text: Government of Ireland International Education Scholarship (Ireland). Ireland offers government and higher education scholarships for international students. The Government of Ireland International Education Scholarship is a competitive scholarship route for high-caliber international students studying in Ireland. Applicants should check Education in Ireland and the relevant Irish higher education institution for eligible programmes, award value, application process, and deadlines.
scholarship: Government of Ireland International Education Scholarship
degree_level: Bachelor's / Master's / PhD depending on programme
language_details: English proficiency required
funding_details: Typically provides a stipend and full fee waiver for one year, depending on the specific award rules.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Generally open to Mongolian applicants or international applicants, but applicants should verify current official eligibility rules.

text: JICA Scholarships. JICA offers long-term training and scholarship programmes connected to Japan's development cooperation, including programmes for government officials, researchers, and future leaders from partner countries. Examples include SDGs Global Leader and JDS-related programmes in some countries. These opportunities often support master's or doctoral study in Japan and are commonly linked to public sector capacity building, development policy, and human resource development. Eligibility depends strongly on country office, government nomination, and programme type.
language_details: English or Japanese depending on program
funding_details: Typically covers tuition, living allowance, travel, and related training expenses depending on programme.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Eligibility depends on country office, government nomination, and program type.

text: Rotary Peace Fellowship. The Rotary Peace Fellowship supports leaders with work experience in peace and development. Rotary awards fellowships for master's degree programmes and professional development certificate programmes at Rotary Peace Centers. The master's programme usually lasts 15 to 24 months and includes applied field experience, while the certificate programme is intended for experienced working professionals.
language_details: English proficiency required
funding_details: Typically covers tuition and fees, room and board, transportation, and internship or field-study expenses.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Generally open to Mongolian applicants or international applicants, but applicants should verify current official eligibility rules.

text: AAUW International Fellowships. AAUW provides fellowship and grant opportunities that support women in education and career advancement. International Fellowships have historically supported women who are not U.S. citizens or permanent residents for graduate or postgraduate study in the United States, though applicants should verify the current cycle, eligibility, and award rules on the AAUW fellowships page.S. study, gender equity, and non-U.S. citizens seeking fellowship support.
language_details: English proficiency required
funding_details: Provides fellowship funding for study or research; usually not a complete full-cost scholarship.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Generally open to Mongolian applicants or international applicants, but applicants should verify current official eligibility rules.

text: Google Generation Scholarship. The Generation Google Scholarship supports students pursuing computer science or related technical degrees, with regional versions such as Asia Pacific depending on the cycle. Applicants are usually evaluated on academic background, leadership, and commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion in technology. Eligibility varies by region and year.
degree_level: Undergraduate / Computer science depending on region
language_details: Depends on region; English generally expected
funding_details: Provides a fixed scholarship award amount; does not usually cover full tuition and living costs.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Eligibility varies by region and application cycle.

text: Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program. The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program partners with universities and organizations to support talented young people, especially from Africa and other eligible contexts depending on partner rules, to access education and leadership development. The programme often combines scholarship support with mentoring, leadership training, transition support, and community impact goals. Eligibility depends on the partner institution and programme.
degree_level: Secondary / Undergraduate / Master's depending on partner
language_details: Depends on partner institution requirements
funding_details: Often covers tuition, accommodation, books, living support, mentoring, and other academic support, depending on partner institution.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Eligibility depends on partner institution and country/region rules; many routes are Africa-focused.

text: Vanier Canada Graduate Scholarships (Canada). The Vanier Canada Graduate Scholarships program aims to attract and retain world-class doctoral students by supporting individuals who demonstrate leadership skills and a high standard of scholarly achievement in graduate studies. The scholarship is available to both Canadian and international students pursuing PhD degrees at participating Canadian institutions.
language_details: English or French depending on institution
funding_details: Provides a fixed annual doctoral scholarship amount; may not cover all costs depending on institution and living expenses.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Generally open to Mongolian applicants or international applicants, but applicants should verify current official eligibility rules.

text: Knight-Hennessy Scholars (USA). The Knight-Hennessy Scholars program at Stanford University supports graduate students from around the world in multiple disciplines. It emphasizes leadership, civic commitment, and independence of thought. Scholars receive funding for graduate study at Stanford and participate in leadership development programming.S. graduate education, interdisciplinary programs, leadership development, and elite university scholarships.
language_details: English proficiency required for Stanford admission
funding_details: Typically funds graduate study at Stanford including tuition, stipend, and related academic expenses.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Generally open to Mongolian applicants or international applicants, but applicants should verify current official eligibility rules.

text: Gates Cambridge Scholarship (United Kingdom). The Gates Cambridge Scholarship supports outstanding applicants from outside the United Kingdom to pursue a full-time postgraduate degree at the University of Cambridge. The program selects scholars based on academic excellence, leadership potential, and commitment to improving the lives of others.
language_details: English proficiency required
funding_details: Typically covers university composition fee, maintenance allowance, airfare, visa, and health surcharge.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Generally open to Mongolian applicants or international applicants, but applicants should verify current official eligibility rules.

text: Schwarzman Scholars (China). The Schwarzman Scholars program is a highly selective international scholarship program at Tsinghua University in Beijing. It focuses on leadership, global affairs, and China studies. The program is conducted in English and brings together students from around the world for a one-year master's degree.
language_details: Program is conducted in English; proficiency required
funding_details: Typically covers tuition, room and board, travel, books and supplies, health insurance, and stipend.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Generally open to Mongolian applicants or international applicants, but applicants should verify current official eligibility rules.

text: Rhodes Scholarship (United Kingdom). The Rhodes Scholarship is one of the oldest and most prestigious international scholarship programs, enabling outstanding students from around the world to study at the University of Oxford. Eligibility depends on specific country quotas, and applicants must demonstrate academic excellence, leadership, and commitment to service.
language_details: English proficiency required
funding_details: Typically covers Oxford course fees, annual stipend, and travel support.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Eligibility depends on country constituency and route; applicants must verify whether Mongolia is included.

text: ADB Internship Program (Asian Development Bank). The ADB Internship Program provides opportunities for students pursuing postgraduate studies to gain experience through research assignments and professional work at the Asian Development Bank. It is not a full scholarship but offers exposure to development work and international organizations.
language_details: Strong English proficiency required
funding_details: Internship program rather than scholarship; may provide stipend or allowance but not full degree funding.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Generally open to Mongolian applicants or international applicants, but applicants should verify current official eligibility rules.

text: Fulbright U.S. Student Program (United States citizens only). The Fulbright U.S. Student Program provides grants for U.S. citizens to study, research, or teach English abroad. This is different from the Fulbright Foreign Student Program for Mongolian applicants. Mongolian citizens are not eligible unless they are also U.S. citizens or U.S. nationals. Permanent residents of the United States are not eligible for this U.S. Fulbright route.
degree_level: Bachelor's-level graduates / Research / English teaching / Graduate study depending on award
language_details: Depends on country and award; some awards require host-country language ability.
funding_details: Typically provides grant support for travel, living expenses, and project or study costs; exact benefits vary by award.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Restricted to U.S. citizens or U.S. nationals; most Mongolian citizens are not eligible.

text: Marshall Scholarship (United Kingdom, U.S. citizens only). The Marshall Scholarship finances young Americans of high ability to study for a graduate degree in the United Kingdom. It is a prestigious scholarship, but it is restricted to U.S. citizens. Mongolian citizens are not eligible unless they also hold U.S. citizenship and meet the U.S. degree requirements.
language_details: English proficiency is required because study takes place at UK universities.
funding_details: Typically covers university fees, living costs, travel, and other approved expenses.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Restricted to U.S. citizens; Mongolian citizens are not eligible unless they also hold U.S. citizenship.

text: George J. Mitchell Scholarship (Ireland, U.S. citizens only). The George J. Mitchell Scholarship supports future American leaders for one year of graduate study in Ireland or Northern Ireland. It is prestigious but restricted to U.S. citizens. Mongolian citizens are not eligible unless they are also U.S. citizens.
language_details: English proficiency is required for graduate study in Ireland or Northern Ireland.
funding_details: Typically covers tuition, accommodation, stipend, and travel support.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Restricted to U.S. citizens; most Mongolian citizens are not eligible.

text: Churchill Scholarship (United Kingdom, U.S. citizens only). The Churchill Scholarship supports one year of graduate study in science, mathematics, and engineering at Churchill College, University of Cambridge. It is highly selective and restricted to U.S. citizens from participating institutions. Mongolian citizens are not eligible unless they are also U.S. citizens and meet the participating institution requirements.
language_details: English proficiency is required for study at Cambridge.
funding_details: Typically covers tuition, living allowance, travel, and related study costs.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Restricted to U.S. citizens from participating institutions.

text: Boren Awards (United States citizens only). Boren Scholarships and Fellowships fund U.S. students to study languages and regions important to U.S. national security. The awards are designed for U.S. citizens and include a federal service requirement. Mongolian citizens are not eligible unless they are also U.S. citizens.
degree_level: Undergraduate / Graduate fellowship depending on route
language_details: Language study is central to the award; applicants propose language-focused study.
funding_details: Provides scholarship or fellowship funding for study abroad; amount depends on award type and duration.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Restricted to U.S. citizens.

text: Benjamin A. Gilman International Scholarship (United States students only). The Gilman Scholarship supports American undergraduate students with high financial need to study or intern abroad. It is a U.S. Department of State program intended for U.S. students. Mongolian citizens are not eligible unless they are studying as eligible U.S. students and meet the citizenship and federal aid requirements.
language_details: Language study may be part of the program but is not always required.
funding_details: Provides a scholarship award for study or internship abroad; does not normally cover all costs.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Restricted to eligible U.S. undergraduate students, usually Pell Grant recipients.

text: NSF Graduate Research Fellowship Program (United States citizens, nationals, or permanent residents). The NSF Graduate Research Fellowship Program supports outstanding graduate students in research-based master's and doctoral degrees in STEM fields. It is a major U.S. fellowship but is restricted by citizenship or permanent residency. Mongolian citizens are not eligible unless they are U.S. citizens, U.S. nationals, or U.S. permanent residents.
language_details: English proficiency is required for U.S. graduate study, but the fellowship itself is primarily citizenship- and field-based.
funding_details: Provides annual stipend and cost-of-education allowance for eligible graduate study.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Eligible only if the applicant is a U.S. citizen, U.S. national, or U.S. permanent resident.

text: The Gates Scholarship (United States high school seniors only). The Gates Scholarship supports outstanding low-income high school seniors in the United States. It is different from Gates Cambridge and is not an international graduate scholarship. Mongolian citizens are not eligible unless they are U.S. citizens, nationals, or permanent residents and meet the U.S. high school and Pell eligibility requirements.
language_details: English proficiency is expected because the program is for U.S. undergraduate study.
funding_details: Last-dollar scholarship support toward the full cost of attendance not already covered by other financial aid and family contribution.
mongolia_eligibility_note: Eligible only if the applicant meets U.S. citizenship or residency and U.S. high school requirements.
